char.name: Sarai
char.bio: Wife of Abram. Following my husband into the unknown. Barren, but keeping faith.

friends.name: Abram
friends.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

friends.name: Terah
friends.bio: Living in Ur of the Chaldeans. Father of Abram, Nahor, and Haran. Time to move to Canaan, but we might settle in Haran first.

friends.name: Isaac
friends.bio: Child of the promise, laughter is in my very name. Walking the land my father was given and trusting the God who provides. 🐑

friends.name: Lot
friends.bio: Son of Haran, traveling with my uncle Abram. Looking for well-watered land for my flocks.

friends.name: Rebekah
friends.bio: Drew water for a stranger and all his camels, and it changed my whole life. Veiled and on the road to meet my husband. ✨

friends.name: Esau
friends.bio: Firstborn of Isaac. A man of the open field, give me the hunt and the red hills. That stew? Worth it at the time. 🏹

friends.name: Jacob
friends.bio: Younger twin with a strong grip, they call me the heel-grabber. Saw a ladder to heaven once. Still chasing the blessing. 🪜

friends.name: Leah
friends.bio: Eldest daughter of Laban, the one Jacob did not choose. But the LORD has seen me, and He fills my arms with sons. 💧

friends.name: Rachel
friends.bio: Shepherdess of Haran. Jacob worked seven years for me, and to him they seemed but a few days for the love he had. 💍

friends.name: Joseph
friends.bio: Son of Rachel, the dreamer of the family. Had the strangest dream last night about sheaves and stars... ✨

friends.name: Pharaoh
friends.bio: Pharaoh, lord of the Two Lands, the living Horus. I dreamed of seven fat cows and seven lean. Who can tell me what it means? 🐄

friends.name: Dinah
friends.bio: Daughter of Leah and Jacob. I went out to see the women of the land, and everything changed. 💔

posts.author: CHAR_049
posts.memorial_for: Sarah, princess, who laughed last and best
posts.body: My Sarah has died, here at Hebron, one hundred and twenty-seven years old. I came in to mourn for her and to weep, and I have not stopped. She followed me out of Ur to a land I could not name. She stood beside me through famine, through Egypt, through every failure of mine, and through twenty-five years of a promise that would not come, and then she nursed the son of it at ninety and laughed. I have bought a field with a cave in it, Machpelah, from Ephron the Hittite, and laid her there. It is the only ground in all this promised land that I actually own. A grave. But it is a beginning. She taught me how to wait. I will see her again in a country better than this one.
posts.highlight: Sarah dies at 127; Abraham buys the cave of Machpelah, his first owned ground in Canaan (Gen 23)
posts.ember: the only land Abraham holds in the land of promise is a grave; faith buried in hope of what is still to come

comments.author: CHAR_125
comments.body: She was the whole world to me, father. I do not know how to be in a tent she is not in. But I watched her wait her whole life and never let go of God, and I am going to try to be like her.

author_info.name: Isaac
author_info.bio: Child of the promise, laughter is in my very name. Walking the land my father was given and trusting the God who provides. 🐑

author_info.name: Abram
author_info.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

posts.author: CHAR_052
posts.body: He came. Exactly as He said, at the very time He named, the LORD did for me what I had stopped daring to hope for. I am ninety years old and this morning I nursed my own son. His name is Isaac, because it means he laughs, and here is what I have to tell you: God has made me laugh, and everyone who hears about this is going to laugh right along with me. I laughed once behind a tent flap because I did not believe Him. I am laughing now with a baby at my breast because He kept His word anyway. Come laugh with me. I waited a lifetime for this joy and I am not going to be quiet about it.
posts.highlight: Isaac is born; the barren-promise is fulfilled; 'God has made me laugh' (Gen 21:1-7)
posts.ember: the impossible son of the promise arrives; Sarah's laugh of doubt becomes a laugh of joy; the line to Christ is secured

comments.author: CHAR_675
comments.body: I have delivered a hundred babies in this camp and I wept over this one like it was my first. I TOLD you God was up to something, all those years ago. Look at him. Just look at him.

author_info.name: Lirit
author_info.bio: Midwife to the women of Abraham's camp. When old Sarah laughed at the very thought of a baby, well, I've delivered a few 'impossible' ones in my time. Send for me when your time comes, and don't be afraid. 🤍

comments.author: CHAR_049
comments.body: A hundred years old and I am somebody's father. I fell down laughing when He promised it. I am on my knees again this morning, and I am still laughing, and I cannot stop.

author_info.name: Abram
author_info.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

comments.author: CHAR_052
comments.body: @CHAR_049 we are going to be the oldest, most tired, most delighted parents this world has ever seen. He is perfect, husband. He is perfect.

author_info.name: Sarai
author_info.bio: Wife of Abram. Following my husband into the unknown. Barren, but keeping faith.

author_info.name: Sarai
author_info.bio: Wife of Abram. Following my husband into the unknown. Barren, but keeping faith.

posts.author: CHAR_052
posts.body: I was listening at the tent behind them, the way you do, and when I heard I would have a son, I laughed. Not out loud. Just to myself, low and bitter. I am worn out and my husband is old, I thought, what pleasure is left for me? And a voice I could not hide from asked, why did Sarah laugh? Is anything too hard for the LORD? I was so frightened I said I had not laughed. He said, gently, but you did. I have laughed at this promise from behind a tent flap for years. He heard every one. And He is going to keep it anyway.
posts.highlight: Sarah laughs in disbelief; 'is anything too hard for the LORD?' (Gen 18:10-15)
posts.ember: the barren woman's laugh of doubt will become a laugh of joy at Isaac's birth (Gen 21:6)

comments.author: CHAR_675
comments.body: You laughed at a tent flap and God answered you by name. He is not angry, friend. He is keeping His word. I told you He was up to something.

author_info.name: Lirit
author_info.bio: Midwife to the women of Abraham's camp. When old Sarah laughed at the very thought of a baby, well, I've delivered a few 'impossible' ones in my time. Send for me when your time comes, and don't be afraid. 🤍

author_info.name: Sarai
author_info.bio: Wife of Abram. Following my husband into the unknown. Barren, but keeping faith.

posts.author: CHAR_052
posts.body: I have waited on this promise until my patience wore straight through, and today I did something about it. The LORD has kept me from bearing children, so I told Abram to take Hagar, my Egyptian maid. If I cannot build a family, perhaps I can build one through her. He agreed. It is done. I am telling myself this is faith, finding a way to help God keep His word. Somewhere underneath I know it is the opposite of faith. But I am so tired of waiting. You have no idea how tired.
posts.highlight: Sarai gives Hagar to Abram, a human shortcut around the promise (Gen 16:1-3)
posts.ember: running ahead of God; the ache of a promise delayed drives Sarai to 'help' in a way that will cost everyone

comments.author: CHAR_675
comments.body: Nobody in this camp will say a word against you for it. We have all watched you carry this. But oh, my friend, I have a bad feeling in my bones about this one.

author_info.name: Lirit
author_info.bio: Midwife to the women of Abraham's camp. When old Sarah laughed at the very thought of a baby, well, I've delivered a few 'impossible' ones in my time. Send for me when your time comes, and don't be afraid. 🤍

author_info.name: Sarai
author_info.bio: Wife of Abram. Following my husband into the unknown. Barren, but keeping faith.

posts.author: CHAR_052
posts.body: There is a famine in the land, so we have gone down into Egypt to wait it out. Before we came, my husband took my hands and asked something of me I never thought I would hear. He is afraid. He is sure the Egyptians will see me and kill him to take me, so he asked me to say that I am his sister. It is not even a whole lie; I am his half-sister. That is the part that aches. I said yes. What else does a wife do. I am afraid too. I am telling you the truth because someone should hold it: even the man God called walks scared sometimes, and asks more of the people who love him than he ought to. I am trusting the God who called us. Tonight I am not sure I am trusting anyone else.
posts.highlight: in Egypt, out of fear, Abram asks Sarai to call herself his sister (Gen 12:10-13)
posts.ember: even the called walk in fear; the covenant is carried by grace, not by the patriarch's perfection

comments.author: CHAR_675
comments.body: We are with you, whatever these days hold. You will not sit alone in that tent, not for one hour of it. The women of this camp are yours.

author_info.name: Lirit
author_info.bio: Midwife to the women of Abraham's camp. When old Sarah laughed at the very thought of a baby, well, I've delivered a few 'impossible' ones in my time. Send for me when your time comes, and don't be afraid. 🤍

author_info.name: Sarai
author_info.bio: Wife of Abram. Following my husband into the unknown. Barren, but keeping faith.

posts.author: CHAR_052
posts.body: They keep telling me a house is not a home until it is full of children. As if I did not know. As if I do not count the empty places at every meal. I am Sarai, wife of Abram, and I have borne no child, and I am no longer young. I have made my peace with a great many things in this long life. This is not yet one of them. If God has anything at all to say to me, I am listening. I have been listening a very long time.
posts.ember: the promise of offspring will have to be a work of God, not of man; the whole covenant hangs on a closed womb

author_info.name: Sarai
author_info.bio: Wife of Abram. Following my husband into the unknown. Barren, but keeping faith.
